tool_output.output:
# Starlink Constellation Size Breakdown

Based on the provided text chunks, here is what can be pieced together:

## Total Constellation
The texts consistently reference **approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites** in Low-Earth Orbit as of March 31, 2026. This 9,600 figure is explicitly described as encompassing **both** broadband and mobile satellites — it is the total combined constellation.

## Mobile (V1 Mobile) Satellites
The text provides a clear growth trajectory for the mobile constellation:
- **January 2025:** approximately **360** V1 Mobile satellites
- **March 31, 2026:** approximately **650** V1 Mobile satellites (described as "dedicated mobile satellites")

## Broadband Satellites
The text does not give a single explicit number for broadband-only satellites in most passages, but one chunk states:

> *"Starlink Consumer Broadband is enabled by the largest satellite constellation in human history with approximately **9,000 broadband satellites** as of March 31, 2026"*

## Implied Breakdown

| Category | Approximate Count |
|----------|------------------|
| **Total** | ~9,600 |
| **Broadband** | ~9,000 |
| **Mobile (V1)** | ~650 |
| **Combined** | ~9,650 |

The slight arithmetic difference (~9,650 vs. ~9,600) likely reflects rounding in the disclosures. The majority of the broadband constellation is composed of **V2 Mini satellites**.

## Key Caveat
The texts note that broadband and mobile satellites are **distinct systems** with different performance requirements and technical specifications, even though they share a similar "V" naming convention.
